critique.summary: The article discusses a game called 'Hostile Volume' that challenges players to maintain a specific volume level through various frustrating and creatively designed puzzles. The game is characterized by its intentionally poor user interface and humorous take on player interaction.
mainPoints: Players must set the volume to exactly 25% and hold it for 3 seconds to progress.
The game features a variety of levels with unique challenges related to volume control.
critique.truthfulness: The article accurately describes the game's premise and mechanics, focusing on the unique challenges players face. However, it lacks external sources or perspectives that would provide additional context or validation of the game's reception.
considerations: The article does not mention any reviews or player feedback from gaming communities.
There is no information about the game's development or the developer's intentions.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the intentionally poor UI design of a volume control game, with users sharing their experiences and thoughts on similar real-world examples. Many comments highlight specific levels and their challenges, while others reflect on the broader implications of bad UI design.

comments.author: dsmason321
comments.text: Level 17 is NOT bugged. The slider is backward and the volume nonresponsive. Its a planned feature.
comments.analysis: This comment provides a clear and accurate explanation of a specific game mechanic, addressing a common misconception.

comments.author: DrSiemer
comments.text: Plenty of annoyance in here for sure. Looks like 17 cannot be finished on mobile though. Switching to desktop view resets progress.
comments.analysis: The comment accurately describes a technical issue related to the game, contributing to the discussion about user experience.

comments.author: pimlottc
comments.text:
This works for almost all levels:

   for (i = 0; i < 50; i++) { document.querySelector("#l3-down").click(); }
comments.analysis: The comment offers a clever workaround for a gameplay challenge, showcasing technical insight and creativity.

comments.author: graypegg
comments.text: This is not an issue at all, but when ever I come across something like it, I like to poke at the frontend in dev tools a bit.
comments.analysis: While the comment provides an interesting perspective on the game mechanics, it downplays the frustrations expressed by others.

comments.author: apublicfrog
comments.text: Great fun, well done to the horrible person who made it. Apparently my RSS reader leaves the browswr live in the background...
comments.analysis: This comment captures a mix of enjoyment and frustration, reflecting the emotional response to the game's design.

comments.author: jimkleiber
comments.text:
Got an error on Level 17, just a heads up.

Love the game, btw.
comments.analysis: While it raises a potential issue, the comment lacks detail and does not provide a solution or deeper insight.

comments.author: Retr0id
comments.text: There are two types of volume slider I've encountered thus far, "too logarithmic", and "not logarithmic enough".
comments.analysis: This comment presents a general observation about volume sliders but lacks specific relevance to the game discussed.

comments.author: jonathanlydall
comments.text: My favourite bad volume control was in Real Player around 1997 where changing the volume in the application actually changed...
comments.analysis: While nostalgic, this comment diverges from the main topic and does not contribute significantly to the current discussion.

comments.author: TheLNL
comments.text: Finished the game. It was fun to play. I got stuck for a while on the opposite level where the display doesn't update...
comments.analysis: The comment shares a personal experience but lacks specific insights about the game's design or mechanics.

comments.author: mdx97
comments.analysis: This comment is overly definitive without providing context or evidence, making it less credible.

comments.author: jupin
comments.text: Laughed out loud but gave up at level 5
comments.analysis: While it expresses a personal reaction, the comment lacks constructive content or insights into the game's design.

comments.author: LoganDark
comments.text: These mostly seem to be variations of "takes a long time / is tedious" rather than "annoying/fiddly / takes skill...
comments.analysis: The comment critiques the game design but does so in a way that may come off as dismissive of others' experiences.

comments.author: Pipe94
comments.text: somehow i'm amazed and annoyed at the same time
comments.analysis: This comment expresses mixed feelings but lacks substance or constructive feedback.

comments.author: burgerone
comments.text: Prwtty neat. Unfortunately wasn't able to solve the UI desync one :/
comments.analysis: While it shares a personal experience, the comment does not provide useful insights or solutions.

comments.author: xnx
comments.text: The worst volume control UI in the world (2017): https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=27819384
comments.analysis: This comment redirects to another link without contributing to the current discussion, making it largely irrelevant.

comments.author: danjl
comments.text: ...and, of course, there's really no need for a volume control in any app, since there's already a system volume...
comments.analysis: This comment dismisses the game's premise without engaging with the topic, rendering it unhelpful.
